summary: postinstall script combines child_process and HTTP primitives — semantic verification needed
details: The package's postinstall.js imports child_process and contains HTTP GET calls and hostname references, a keyword combination commonly seen in install-time droppers but also in benign native-binary fetchers (prebuild-install, vendor SDK download, runtime fetch). Without traced execution evidence, it is not possible to determine from keyword co-occurrence alone whether the fetched bytes are executed as code, written as data, or matched against a publisher-owned destination. Routing for human review to inspect postinstall.js: confirm what URL is fetched, whether the destination matches the package's publisher, whether the response is executed or stored as a data artifact, and whether it modifies installer-side resources outside the package's own directory.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
